Sam Jones — folder mining summary (synthetic fixture for issue #46)

Sam's GitHub profile shows two thematically-related but architecturally-
distinct projects in the LLM tooling space, plus a handful of unrelated
work. This summary covers the two related ones in detail because they're
the shape that surfaced issue #46 — the tailor pre-fix sometimes combined
them into a single H3 heading, which is the wrong polish for a resume.

PROJECT: prompt-harness (github.com/sam/prompt-harness)

A Python CLI tool Sam built and maintains for evaluating prompts across
multiple LLM providers in parallel. The repo has 312 passing tests, MIT
license, and 47 GitHub stars. Architecture:

- Click-based CLI, single entry point `prompt-harness eval --scenarios
  scenarios.yaml`
- Adapter layer for OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini APIs
- Scoring rubric configured per scenario; default uses Claude as judge
  with a weighted 0-10 quality score
- Output is a structured JSON results file plus a Markdown summary table
- Validated 80+ prompts across six scenario categories during the most
  recent benchmarking run (~$5-7 in API spend per full sweep)

Notable artifacts in the repo:
- `harness/adapters/` — one file per LLM backend, ~150 lines each.
  Sam wrote all four adapters; they share a common `BackendAdapter`
  protocol so adding a fifth would be ~50 lines.
- `harness/scoring.py` — the Claude-as-judge rubric. The weighted
  scoring formula is documented inline with examples.
- `tests/fixtures/scenarios/` — 80 scenario files Sam authored over
  six months of iteration.

PROJECT: nonprofit-prompts (github.com/sam/nonprofit-prompts)

A separate static-site project Sam shipped to make a curated subset of
his prompt-harness scenarios publicly browsable. Built on Astro 5.x,
Tailwind v4, React 19, with Pagefind for client-side search and GitHub
Pages for hosting. Has 23 stars and a small but real user base of
nonprofit operators consulting it for AI-tool ideas. Architecture:

- Astro static-site generator. ~120 prompt cards rendered at build time
  from YAML frontmatter sourced from Sam's prompt-harness scenarios.
- React islands for the search UI and the per-prompt comparison table
  (which model gave the highest-scored response).
- Tailwind v4 design system; mobile-first with a max-width 768px layout.
- GitHub Actions CI with link-checker and lighthouse perf gates.

Notable artifacts:
- `src/content/prompts/` — 120 markdown files, one per prompt. Each
  card links back to the prompt-harness GitHub repo for the full
  scenario context. The two repos are linked via these citations but
  are otherwise independent codebases — different stack, different
  deployment, different audience.
- `src/components/CompareTable.tsx` — the React island that renders
  the model-comparison table for each prompt. Reads the harness's
  exported JSON.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — CI pipeline. Runs link-check and
  lighthouse on every PR.

The relationship between the two projects: nonprofit-prompts CONSUMES
the JSON output of prompt-harness's evaluation runs, but they're
separately versioned, separately deployed, and serve different
audiences (developers using the CLI vs. nonprofit operators browsing
the curated subset). They are NOT the same project. They should appear
as two separate `### {Name} ({URL})` blocks in the tailored resume.

OTHER PROJECTS (less detailed; tailor may use or omit at its discretion):
- github.com/sam/feedback-ingest — the customer-feedback service Sam
  shipped at BigCo. Python / FastAPI / Postgres, ~6K stars.
- github.com/sam/dotfiles — personal config repo, not relevant to a
  resume tailored for the AI Product Engineer role.
